articles.title: People battle sweltering heat as mercury soars past 40°C in Doon; Heatwave Alert Issued - uttarakhandnewsnetwork.com
articles.search_term: heatwave (Almora OR Bageshwar OR Chamoli OR Champawat OR Dehradun OR Haridwar OR Nainital OR "Pauri Garhwal" OR Pithoragarh OR Rudraprayag OR "Tehri Garhwal" OR "Udham Singh Nagar" OR Uttarkashi) Uttarakhand
articles.full_text:
Life in the plains of Uttarakhand has been thrown into disarray due to scorching heat. In most regions, including Dehradun, the mercury has soared above 40 degrees Celsius, and relentless heatwaves throughout the day are causing severe distress.
The intense heat is expected to persist in most parts of the state today as well. The Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave warning for the plains.
On Wednesday (May 21), temperatures in the state’s plains were recorded as significantly higher than normal, while temperatures in the hilly regions also remained above average. Over the past 24 hours, maximum temperatures in Haridwar, Pauri, Nainital, Udham Singh Nagar, and Dehradun were recorded at more than five degrees Celsius above normal.
Battling Intense Heat and Heatwaves
Consequently, residents of the plains are having to contend with severe heat and heatwaves. In Dehradun, the intense heat was palpable throughout the day due to the blazing sun. Temperatures have been rising steadily over the past few days. Although no significant change in maximum temperatures is expected over the next two to three days, temperatures could drop by 2 to 3 degrees Celsius in the one or two days following that period.
According to the Meteorological Centre, light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms and showers is likely to occur in isolated pockets across the districts of Uttarkashi, Rudraprayag, Chamoli, Bageshwar, and Pithoragarh.
Yellow Alert Issued for Heatwave
Meanwhile, very light to light rainfall may also occur in some locations within Dehradun, Tehri, and Almora. The weather is expected to remain dry in the remaining districts of the state.
A ‘Yellow Alert’ regarding heatwave conditions has been issued for isolated areas in the lower regions of Haridwar, Pauri, Nainital, Udham Singh Nagar, Dehradun, and Uttarkashi. The administration has advised the public to avoid venturing outdoors unnecessarily during the afternoon hours, to drink adequate amounts of water, and to take special care of children and the elderly.
